_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use comprehension strategies — including inferring — to build literal and inferred meaning from increasingly complex texts.

_source.w: We are learning to use comprehension strategies — including inferring — to build literal and inferred meaning from increasingly complex texts.
y: At this level, much of a text's meaning lies beneath the surface — inferring is how we access it.
Combining multiple comprehension strategies helps us understand texts at the deepest level.
These skills are essential for engaging with the complex texts we encounter across all subjects.
eg: We make inferences about a character's feelings or motivations based on clues in the text.
We use visualising to engage with complex descriptions and abstract ideas.
We monitor our understanding of a complex text and apply fix-up strategies when comprehension breaks down.
We connect ideas across multiple texts to build a richer understanding of a topic.
We ask deep interpretive questions that require us to go beyond the literal meaning.
We summarise complex arguments in our own words, demonstrating accurate comprehension.
